topic: Theme 2: Political and Artistic Culture in the French-speaking World
subject: french

items.correct: A person or place where French is spoken
items.why: Students think 'francophone' means only mainland French people; it covers all French speakers, e.g. in Senegal, Quebec or Belgium.

items.q: The Festival de Cannes is an example of which kind of cultural event in the French-speaking world?
items.why: Students may not recognise major cultural institutions; Cannes is a flagship of francophone artistic culture, not a political event.

items.q: Which is an example of political culture in the French-speaking world?
items.correct: Debates over the use and protection of the French language (e.g. Quebec's language laws)
items.why: Students confuse grammar drills with cultural content; language policy and decolonisation are genuine political-culture themes.

items.q: In which year did Algeria gain independence from France?
items.why: Correct answer: 1962. “1954” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which is NOT part of the Maghreb?
items.why: It's Sénégal. “Algérie” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Quebec's Law 101 (Charter of the French Language) passed?
items.why: Exact answer: 1977. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
